name: Monogate
description: Open-source mathematics stack built on the EML operator eml(x, y) = exp(x) - ln(y). Includes the Pfaffian-cost analyzer (eml-cost) with three prediction functions for SymPy compile-time, float64 numerical precision loss, and PyTorch per-layer activation behavior. Cross-validated against a Lean 4 formalization with 50 EML-original theorems across 14 files (13 of 14 files carry zero sorry; the 14th carries 2 documented sorries pending o-minimal theory).

capabilities.name: EML universality
capabilities.description: The single binary operator eml(x, y) = exp(x) - ln(y) generates every elementary function as a finite tree from the constant 1 alone. Lean-formalized as the IsEMLElementary predicate in Universality.lean.

capabilities.name: Infinite Zeros Barrier (T01)
capabilities.description: Real EML trees have finitely many zeros on any closed bounded interval where they are real-analytic, while sin(x) has infinitely many. Therefore sin is not an element of EML(R). Parts A, B, C, C-prime are closed in Lean. Part D (sin_not_in_eml, sin_not_in_real_EML_k) carries 2 documented sorries pending o-minimal / Wilkie 1996 machinery not yet in Mathlib.

capabilities.name: SuperBEST v5.2 routing table
capabilities.description: Routing table mapping 10 arithmetic operations to F16 expressions, achieving 14n / 80.8% savings on the positive-domain headline (vs the 73n naive baseline) and 22n / 69.9% savings in the general (signed-real) domain. Ten upper-bound constructions are formalized in Lean (UpperBounds.lean).

capabilities.name: SB(add) >= 2
capabilities.description: No single F16 operator equals (x, y) -> x + y for all real x, y. Exhaustive 16-witness refutation in AddLowerBound.lean.

capabilities.name: SB(sub) >= 2
capabilities.description: No single F16 operator equals (x, y) -> x - y for all real x, y. SubLowerBound.lean, 16-case refutation.

capabilities.name: SB(mul) >= 2
capabilities.description: No single F16 operator equals (x, y) -> x * y for all real x, y. MulLowerBound.lean, derivative obstruction route + witness refutation.

capabilities.name: SB(div) >= 2
capabilities.description: No single F16 operator equals (x, y) -> x / y for all real x, y. DivLowerBound.lean.

capabilities.name: estimate_time(expr) compile-time predictor
capabilities.description: Predicts SymPy compile wall-time across simplify/factor/cse/lambdify proxies from the Pfaffian profile. Regression model fit on 202 expressions (cross-domain-3 R v2). 5-fold CV R^2 by proxy: simplify 0.68, factor 0.76, cse 0.83, lambdify 0.84.

capabilities.name: predict_precision_loss(expr) runtime numerical predictor
capabilities.description: Predicts float64 numerical relerr versus 50-digit mpmath ground truth. Fit on 379 expressions from the bench-300-domain corpus. 5-fold CV R^2 = +0.271 +/- 0.060, residual log10 std = 0.772, marginal eml_depth coefficient +0.337 (~0.34 decimal digits per depth unit). Modest signal explicitly NOT used as a form recommender (E-193 Phase 3 best-pick was 30 percent on rewrite tests, below the 70 percent product threshold).

capabilities.name: diagnose(model) per-layer activation behavior (eml-cost-torch)
capabilities.description: Per-layer per-activation lookup of fp16 drift and activation-variance for 19 named PyTorch activation classes. Five seeds per activation, ranked from GeGLU (high drift ~7.4e-4) to Hardsigmoid (low drift ~2.3e-4). The earlier PNE-vs-EML generalization (E-183) was empirically falsified on a controlled corpus (p=0.085); honest_note baked into report.empirical_basis.

capabilities.name: eml-cost CLI
capabilities.description: Console command exposing analyze + estimate_time + predict_precision_loss as `eml-cost report` and `eml-cost check`. Pre-commit usable via --max-simplify-ms and --max-digits-lost budgets. Exit codes: 0 OK, 1 threshold violation, 2 parse error, 64 usage.

capabilities.name: JavaScript port via monogate/cost subpath
capabilities.description: JavaScript port of analyze + predict_precision_loss in npm monogate 1.3.0, with byte-identical Python coefficients. Cost-class fingerprint matches Python eml-cost on 32 of 32 reference cases. predict_precision_loss matches byte-for-byte on 14 of 20 representative cases; remaining 6 differ by up to about a factor of 10 due to SymPy automatic canonicalizations on the JS-parsed literal tree.

capabilities.name: p2-d5-w2-c1 cost-class enrichment in the bench-300-domain corpus
capabilities.description: Across the 403-expression cross-domain corpus and N_null=5000 random expressions, cost class p2-d5-w2-c1 shows 2.7x enrichment with q < 1e-4 after Benjamini-Hochberg correction across 31 candidate classes, spanning 18 distinct domains. Earlier headline of 'binary 0/50 null with >= 40x enrichment' was a small-N artifact and is corrected here.

benchmarks.name: SuperBEST v5.2 routing table values

reproducibility.command: cat blog/src/data/superbest.json | python -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d['totals']['positive_headline'])"

benchmarks.notes: Headline: 14n positive (80.8 percent vs 73n naive). General domain: 22n (69.9 percent). Source: blog/src/data/superbest.json v5.2.

benchmarks.name: Lean 4 verification (Mathlib v4.14.0)

reproducibility.command: git clone https://github.com/agent-maestro/monogate-lean && cd monogate-lean && lake build

benchmarks.notes: 13 of 14 files in MonogateEML/ build with zero sorries. The 14th (InfiniteZerosBarrier.lean) carries 2 active sorries on Part D (lines 326 and 337), both documented as requiring o-minimal theory.

benchmarks.name: eml-cost test suite

reproducibility.command: pip install eml-cost==0.7.1 && git clone https://github.com/agent-maestro/eml-cost && cd eml-cost && python -m pytest

benchmarks.notes: Includes 26 tests for predict_precision_loss and 16 tests for the CLI. mypy --strict clean across 11 source files.

benchmarks.name: eml-cost-torch test suite

reproducibility.command: pip install eml-cost-torch==0.5.0 && git clone https://github.com/agent-maestro/eml-cost-torch && cd eml-cost-torch && python -m pytest

benchmarks.notes: Per-activation empirical lookup tests + diagnose() integration tests.

benchmarks.name: monogate npm test suite

reproducibility.command: npm install monogate@1.3.0 && cd node_modules/monogate && npx vitest run

benchmarks.notes: Across 5 test files: cost.test.js (68), cost_precision.test.js (37), complex.test.js (38), complex_eml.test.js (71), index.test.js (215).

benchmarks.name: Cost-class p2-d5-w2-c1 enrichment vs random null

reproducibility.command: git clone https://github.com/agent-maestro/monogate-research-public-bench && cd bench-300-domain && python stats.py --n_null 5000

benchmarks.notes: Across the 403-expression bench-300-domain corpus and N_null=5000. Benjamini-Hochberg FDR-corrected q < 1e-4 across 31 candidate classes. 18 distinct domains contribute. The publication of this benchmark expects the public bench repo to be available; until then the corpus and stats.py live in monogate-research/exploration/bench-300-domain/.

proofs.name: SB(add) >= 2
proofs.statement: No single F16 operator computes x + y for all real x and y.
proofs.proof_method: Witness-based case analysis over all 16 F16 operators; paired with a 2-node upper-bound construction.

proofs.name: SB(sub) >= 2
proofs.statement: No single F16 operator computes x - y for all real x and y.

proofs.name: SB(mul) >= 2
proofs.statement: No single F16 operator computes x * y for all real x and y.

proofs.name: SB(div) >= 2
proofs.statement: No single F16 operator computes x / y for all real x and y.

proofs.name: EML universality witness
proofs.statement: Every EML-elementary function is realized by an EML routing tree.

proofs.name: Infinite Zeros Barrier (T01) — partial
proofs.statement: Real EML trees have finitely many zeros on any closed bounded interval where they are real-analytic. Parts A, B, C, C-prime fully closed; Part D (sin not in EML) carries 2 documented sorries.
proofs.proof_method: Bolzano-Weierstrass + AnalyticOnNhd identity theorem (Parts B and C); Part D requires o-minimal / Wilkie 1996 machinery not yet in Mathlib.
proofs.sorries_description: sin_not_in_eml and sin_not_in_real_EML_k pending a quantitative o-minimal zero-count bound not yet formalized in Mathlib v4.14.0.

verification.evidence_summary: 13 of 14 Lean files build clean with zero sorries. The 14th file (InfiniteZerosBarrier.lean) carries 2 active sorries on Part D, both documented as requiring o-minimal theory not yet in Mathlib. Six independent benchmark categories ship with reproducible commands. Tests across all five published packages currently pass.

honest_limitations: predict_precision_loss is NOT a form recommender; cost class does not reliably pick the more numerically stable form within an algebraic equivalence class (E-193 Phase 3 best-pick = 30 percent across 10 rewrite tests).

Form-sensitivity: about 50 percent of textbook expressions yield different cost classes for algebraically equivalent forms. canonicalize() preprocessing reduces this to about 35 percent. The remaining drift is documented.

Real-world (bio / physics / engineering) expressions are NOT specially numerically clean compared to random nulls at matched cost class (pooled diff = -0.001 log10 across 4 matched classes; E-193 Phase 4).
diagnose(model) per-activation drift values are measured on a single fixed FFN; per-architecture variation is not yet characterized.
JS port of predict_precision_loss matches Python byte-for-byte on 14 of 20 representative cases; 6 of 20 differ by up to about a factor of 10 due to SymPy automatic canonicalization differences.

metadata.rewrite_note: Rewritten 2026-04-27 to align with verified CONTEXT.md numbers, drop earlier boasting framing, and remove an unverified manual trust score. All quantitative claims are now traceable to a reproducible command or a Lean source file.
